VLP Battery — Agile Consumer Model

The consumer stays on Octopus Agile and benefits from battery arbitrage on Agile rates. The company operates the battery as a Virtual Lead Party (VLP), earning grid services revenue (wholesale arbitrage + FR + CM + BM). No supply cost, no network charges, no grid levy.

Adjust Inputs Charts are static — tables & dashboard update live
Business model: Consumer keeps their Agile tariff and gets battery savings from arbitrage (charge at low Agile rates, discharge during expensive hours). The VLP company earns wholesale arbitrage, frequency response, capacity market, and balancing mechanism revenue on the remaining battery capacity. No electricity supply licence needed — no levies, no network charges for the company.

Battery Revenue Breakdown (Year 1)

The MILP jointly optimises consumer savings and VPP income hour-by-hour. Higher consumption = more consumer demand hours = more savings from Agile arbitrage, but less grid discharge available for VPP wholesale income.

ConsumptionConsumer Savings £Wholesale Arb £ FR £CM £BM £Total VPP £Total Value £
4,000 kWh/yr 546 105 34 50 1 189 735
5,000 kWh/yr 700 60 24 50 0 134 834
6,000 kWh/yr 792 35 42 50 0 128 920

Key Parameters

Battery Hardware

ParameterValueNotes
Capacity15.36 kWhUsable LFP cells
Inverter power6 kWMax charge/discharge rate
Round-trip efficiency90%One-way: 94.87%
Total cost£2,800Hardware + installation
Economic life10 yearsStraight-line depreciation: £280/yr
Annual degradation2%/yrYr5: 92.2%, Yr10: 83.4%

Financing

ParameterValue
Battery financingConsumer pays upfront — no company loan or depreciation
Corporation tax25%
Model horizon12 years

Grid Services Revenue

ServiceRateAnnual (full battery)
Frequency Response (FR)£34/kW/yr£204 max
Capacity Market (CM)£40/kW/yr£50 (de-rated 20.9%)
Balancing Mechanism (BM)10% win x 5% upliftVaries

VLP Company Cost Stack (per customer/yr)

Cost lineAmountNotes
Staff cost£50Annual per customer
Customer management (VLP)£10From model_input.csv
Grid levy£0VLP is not electricity supplier — no levy
Network charges£0Consumer pays via Agile tariff
Supply cost (COGS)£0Company does not supply electricity
CAC (year 1 only)£100Customer acquisition cost
Total annual opex£60

Assumptions & Limitations

1. Consumer stays on Octopus Agile. Consumer savings are based on the Agile tariff spread (charge low, discharge high). If the consumer switches tariff, savings would differ. Agile rates may change structurally in future years.
2. Perfect foresight on prices. The MILP optimises each day's schedule using actual prices. A real dispatch algorithm would achieve 10-30% lower returns.
3. Battery degradation at 2%/yr. All revenue (consumer savings + VPP income) is scaled by degradation factor each year.
4. Grid services assumed contractually secured. FR and CM revenues require competitive tender wins. Actual DC rates have fallen recently.
5. BM estimated, not modelled. BM revenue = 10% x 5% uplift on wholesale arbitrage.
6. VLP has no supply obligations. The company is a Virtual Lead Party, not an electricity supplier. No Ofgem supply licence needed, no grid levy, no network charges, no BSUoS/DUoS/TNUoS. Consumer bears all supply costs via their Agile tariff.
7. Same spread compression as electricity company model. Agile rate spreads compress at the same rate as wholesale spreads. In practice, Agile spreads may compress differently as retail tariff methodology evolves.
8. One cycle per day limit. The MILP constrains total daily charge and discharge to one battery capacity. This is conservative but protects battery life.
9. CM stress events assumed rare. Battery is usually available for other activities alongside CM obligations.
10. Consumer pays for battery. The P&L and cashflow assume the consumer funds the battery upfront. The company has no depreciation, no loan repayments, and no interest expense. Only ongoing costs are staff and VLP customer management.
11. Corp tax simplified at 25%. No loss carry-forward, group relief, or capital allowances.
12. UK use profile applied. Hourly consumption shape from use_profiles.csv (UK), scaled proportionally.
13. EUR/GBP fixed at 0.85. Wholesale prices in EUR/MWh converted at fixed rate.
Generated by VLP Battery — Agile Consumer Model | Agile rates 2025 | UK wholesale Jul 2024 - Jun 2025
